1.1.section: 1. Data description and collection or re-use of existing data
1.1.question: How will new data be collected or produced and/or how will existing data be re-used?
1.1.content:
All new experimental data will be collected using specialised software dedicated to the measuring device used, e.g. laser vibrometer (Polytec PSV400, POLYTEC MSA-500). Simulation data will be generated during computer simulations using COMSOL, Matlab and Phyton software.The data will consist of the results of numerical and experimental analyses in the form of: vibration amplitudes, displacements, geometrical features and coordinates of nodes.
All new data will be processed and collected directly by the researchers on their workstations and external network drive.
Research procedures, will be collected by PI in a notebook. During the project, existing data will also be used. The data to be used are available under specific restrictions, which we will follow in our project the following licence types: CC-BY or CC-BY-NC.

1.2.section: 1. Data description and collection or re-use of existing data
1.2.question: What data (for example the types, formats, and volumes) will be collected or produced?
1.2.content:
The full data set for a single measurement/simulation process is approximately 2 GB.
The following formats will be used: .txt and .svd (measurement) and .csv, .dat, .mat, . mph and .py (simulations).
All data selected for long-term archiving and sharing, will be in suitable open format like: txt, pdf, png. It is expected that the maximum size of all data generated during the project will not exceed 48TB.
Dokumentacja i jakość danych

2.1.question: What metadata and documentation (for example methodology or data collection and way of organising data) will accompany data?
2.1.content:
We will document more metadata than needed for reproducibility. We will use README files to document data origin and generation/processing steps. Description of our folder organisation :
Project_name/Exp_name/Date/sample_name.txt. Selected data will be facilitated by open research data repository.
The MOST Wiedzy Open Research Catalog from Gdańsk University of Technology with metadata standards such as DataCite. Metadata description will be stored in JSON-LD format. Contributor will be identified and authorized by an ORCID number.

The MOST Wiedzy Open Research Catalog from Gdańsk University of Technology with metadata standards such as DataCite. Metadata description will be stored in JSON-LD format. Contributor will be identified and authorized by an ORCID number.

2.2.question: What data quality control measures will be used?
2.2.content: High care of sample characterization will be undertaken fulfilling the FAIR standards. Data will be mostly created and collected automatically. If needed, we will define a way to detect particular files independently. The storage of data on a server assigned to the project will also allow for manual checking and verification of data.

3.1.section: 3. Storage and backup during the research process
3.1.question: How will data and metadata be stored and backed up during the research process?
3.1.content: The created and collected data in raw form and as result of analysis or postprocessing will be stored on Gdańsk Tech bussiness computer/workstation and shared workspace. All data derived from simulations will be also stored on PCs. The backup process will be done monthly and backup files will be stored on external hard drive and shared workspace. This workspace will be run by dedicated specialists.

tagged_paragraphs.text: The created and collected data in raw form and as result of analysis or postprocessing will be stored on Gdańsk Tech bussiness computer/workstation and shared workspace. All data derived from simulations will be also stored on PCs. The backup process will be done monthly and backup files will be stored on external hard drive and shared workspace. This workspace will be run by dedicated specialists.

3.2.section: 3. Storage and backup during the research process
3.2.question: How will data security and protection of sensitive data be taken care of during the research?
3.2.content: No sensitive data will be collected during the project. Only project members will have read access; only selected project members will be able to write data. Intranet disc space (array) governed by Gdańsk Tech and protected by passwords, will be used for sharing data with coworkers - if necessary. The backup of all the data will be done during the whole project duration.

The backup of all the data will be done during the whole project duration.

4.1.question: If personal data are processed, how will compliance with legislation on personal data and on data security be ensured?
4.1.content: We don't need any consent for collected data because these are not personal.

tagged_paragraphs.text: We don't need any consent for collected data because these are not personal.

4.2.question: How will other legal issues, such as intellectual property rights and ownership, be managed? What legislation is applicable?

5.1.question: How and when will data be shared? Are there possible restrictions to data sharing or embargo reasons?
5.1.content:
The data set will be shared via MOST Wiedzy Open Research Catalog. If needed the part of the data will be published in scientific journals, which may also require raw data publication. Data will be systematically uploaded to the MOST Wiedzy repository in accordance with the publication dates of the articles that are derived from this data.
Only new data, generated during this project, will be published in the MOST Wiedzy repository.

Only new data, generated during this project, will be published in the MOST Wiedzy repository.

5.2.question: How will data for preservation be selected, and where will data be preserved long-term (for example a data repository or archive)?

5.3.question: What methods or software tools will be needed to access and use the data?
5.3.content: The shared data will be in open formats, so there will be no need for specialized software by recipients.

tagged_paragraphs.text: The shared data will be in open formats, so there will be no need for specialized software by recipients.

5.4.question: How will the application of a unique and persistent identifier (such as a Digital Object Identifier (DOI)) to each data set be ensured?
5.4.content: The datasets provided in the repository will have the DOI assigned.

tagged_paragraphs.text: The datasets provided in the repository will have the DOI assigned.

6.1.question: Who (for example role, position, and institution) will be responsible for data management (i.e. the data steward)?
6.1.content: Open Science Competence Center (pg.edu.pl/openscience) - established by Gdańsk Tech will be will be responsible for DMP and quality of metadata descriptions of datasets deposited in MOST Wiedzy repository. Project PI will be responsible for the procedures assessment and overall data quality.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Open Science Competence Center (pg.edu.pl/openscience) - established by Gdańsk Tech will be will be responsible for DMP and quality of metadata descriptions of datasets deposited in MOST Wiedzy repository. Project PI will be responsible for the procedures assessment and overall data quality.

6.2.question: What resources (for example financial and time) will be dedicated to data management and ensuring the data will be FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Re-usable)?
6.2.content: Each member of the research team is expected to follow the data acquisition and storage guidelines. PI will monitor the implementation of the data management plan and, if necessary, make corrections during the project. He will also provide necessary training to the involved students.
